open_prs.purpose: recursive event signature compatibility with Hub

shared_contract.canonicalization: recursive lexicographic object-key ordering; preserve array order; compact UTF-8 JSON
shared_contract.authentication: HMAC-SHA256 with a shared secret of at least 32 characters stored only in approved secret stores

newly_detected_delta.problem: VTI sorted only top-level keys while Hub recursively sorted nested JSON before HMAC verification
newly_detected_delta.impact: Nested event payloads could fail authentication depending on insertion order
newly_detected_delta.resolution: VTI PR #19 recursively canonicalizes JSON and adds a fixed cross-language HMAC vector

cross_chat_rules: Preserve both chat histories and all unique requirements
Do not merge repositories to imitate integration
Assign one canonical owner for each shared requirement
Version breaking schema changes and add compatibility tests
VTI media evidence does not outrank official primary sources
Separate direct token demand from general technology adoption
No financial execution or money movement without separate explicit approval
Update this tracker after every material cross-repository change

priority_queue.action: Wait for and verify VTI PR #19 CI
Review and merge VTI PR #16 only after checks pass and explicit approval
Retarget and merge VTI PR #17, then #18, then #19 in order
Review Hub PR #11 CI and compatibility against VTI #19 vector
Configure staging endpoint and secret references without exposing values
Run an authorised staging event canary from VTI to Hub
Verify idempotency, retry, dead-letter, replay and XRP/HBAR module routing end to end
Surface both service states and evidence IDs in Jarvis Command Centre
Federate approved XRP/HBAR findings to AI CFO recommendation candidates
